items.title: China Chip Stock Pullback Drives Investor Fear to Four-Year High
items.description: A measure of investor sentiment on China technology hardware is flashing its most bearish reading in over four years as the red-hot rally in chip stocks rapidly cools.

items.title: China AI Stocks’ 1000%-Plus Profit Gain May Fail to Revive Rally
items.description: China’s artificial intelligence companies are poised to deliver robust earnings, but elevated expectations after a months-long rally are raising the bar for tech stocks to climb further.

items.title: Thai Stocks Go From Worst to Best as Funds Eye Further Gains
items.description: Thai stocks are finding favor again as investors bet that an Anutin Charnvirakul-led government will bring an end to years of political turbulence, spurring foreign capital inflows.

items.title: Asian Stocks Drop as Chip Selloff Deepens, Oil Up: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian equities fell for a second day as a selloff in chipmakers deepened on mounting concern that massive artificial-intelligence investments may not justify lofty valuations. Oil climbed.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
items.description: Oil headed for its biggest weekly advance since April as the escalating conflict between the US and Iran raised concerns about disruptions to supply from the Middle East.

items.title: CoreLogic in Talks to Sweeten Terms on Struggling Debt Sale
items.description: CoreLogic Inc. is considering offering sweeter terms on at least a portion of its $5.3 billion debt deal after receiving lukewarm investor demand.

items.title: How the Iran War Is Rewiring the Oil Market
items.description: The US-Israel war with Iran was supposed to blow up energy markets, but that hasn’t happened yet. In this weekly documentary, Bloomberg Originals explains why.

items.title: Bonds Have a Big Tech Problem of Their Own
items.description: Concentration risk is becoming a thing.

items.title: Emerging Currencies Fall as US, Iran Tensions Strengthen Dollar
items.description: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as the US launched fresh strikes against Iran, sparking a flight to haven assets that pushed the dollar higher.

items.title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hostilities Rekindles Fed Hike Bets
items.description: Gold declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East rekindled expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to hike interest rates to contain stubborn inflation.
